title: Lab: Cisco Config Parser
description:
Build a Python function that parses Cisco IOS 'show running-config' output and extracts structured interface information. This is a common network automation task — parsing CLI output into machine-readable data.

Your parser should extract interface names, IP addresses, subnet masks, and status for each interface.
starterCode:
import re

def parse_interfaces(config: str) -> list:
    """
    Parse Cisco IOS running config and extract interface information.
    
    Args:
        config: Multi-line string of Cisco 'show running-config' output
    
    Returns:
        List of dictionaries, each with:
        - 'name': Interface name (e.g., 'GigabitEthernet0/0')
        - 'ip_address': IP address or None if not configured
        - 'subnet_mask': Subnet mask or None
        - 'description': Interface description or ''
        - 'shutdown': True if interface is shut down, False otherwise
        - 'vlan': VLAN ID (int) or None for non-VLAN interfaces
    """
    # Your code here
    pass


def find_default_gateway(config: str) -> str:
    """
    Extract the default gateway (ip route 0.0.0.0 0.0.0.0 ...) from config.
    
    Returns:
        Next-hop IP address string, or 'not configured'
    """
    # Your code here
    pass


# Sample Cisco config:
sample_config = '''
hostname Router1
!
interface GigabitEthernet0/0
 description Uplink to ISP
 ip address 203.0.113.2 255.255.255.252
 no shutdown
!
interface GigabitEthernet0/1
 description LAN Connection
 ip address 192.168.1.1 255.255.255.0
 no shutdown
!
interface GigabitEthernet0/2
 description DMZ
 ip address 10.10.10.1 255.255.255.0
 shutdown
!
interface Vlan10
 description Management VLAN
 ip address 172.16.10.1 255.255.255.0
 no shutdown
!
interface Loopback0
 ip address 1.1.1.1 255.255.255.255
 no shutdown
!
ip route 0.0.0.0 0.0.0.0 203.0.113.1
!
'''

interfaces = parse_interfaces(sample_config)
for iface in interfaces:
    status = 'DOWN' if iface['shutdown'] else 'UP'
    ip = iface['ip_address'] or 'no ip'
    print(f"{iface['name']:30s} {ip:20s} {status}  {iface['description']}")

print(f"\nDefault Gateway: {find_default_gateway(sample_config)}")
hints: Split the config into lines and process line-by-line, tracking the current interface block
Use regex: r'^interface\s+(\S+)' to detect interface declarations
Use regex: r'\s+ip address\s+(\S+)\s+(\S+)' to extract IP and mask
A 'shutdown' line (without 'no') means the interface is administratively down
VLAN interfaces have names like 'Vlan10' — extract the number with regex

testCases.description: Finds all 5 interfaces in sample config
testCases.testCode:
config = 'interface GigabitEthernet0/0
 ip address 1.1.1.1 255.255.255.0
!
interface GigabitEthernet0/1
 ip address 2.2.2.2 255.255.255.0
!
interface Vlan10
 ip address 3.3.3.3 255.255.255.0
!'
result = parse_interfaces(config)
len(result) == 3

config = 'interface GigabitEthernet0/0\n ip address 203.0.113.2 255.255.255.252\n!'
result = parse_interfaces(config)
result[0]['ip_address'] == '203.0.113.2'

config = 'interface GigabitEthernet0/0\n ip address 203.0.113.2 255.255.255.252\n!'
result = parse_interfaces(config)
result[0]['subnet_mask'] == '255.255.255.252'

config = 'interface GigabitEthernet0/0
 ip address 1.1.1.1 255.255.255.0
 shutdown
!
interface GigabitEthernet0/1
 ip address 2.2.2.2 255.255.255.0
 no shutdown
!'
result = parse_interfaces(config)
result[0]['shutdown'] == True and result[1]['shutdown'] == False

config = 'interface GigabitEthernet0/0
 description Uplink to ISP
 ip address 1.1.1.1 255.255.255.0
!'
result = parse_interfaces(config)
result[0]['description'] == 'Uplink to ISP'

testCases.description: Detects VLAN interface and extracts VLAN ID
testCases.testCode:
config = 'interface Vlan10\n ip address 172.16.10.1 255.255.255.0\n!'
result = parse_interfaces(config)
result[0]['vlan'] == 10 and result[0]['name'] == 'Vlan10'

testCases.description: Finds default gateway from ip route
Returns 'not configured' when no default route exists
